```{r}

##################
## Package loading
##################

library(snpReady)

```

```{r}

######################################
## Prepare genotypic data for snpReady
######################################

# Read the genotypic data 
geno <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/TdGD80 Genotyping results.csv", header = TRUE)  

## Reformat sample names
# Subset the genotype data 
samples <- geno[, 10:374]  # This creates a dataframe with only sample columns
# Adjust this range based on the structure of the new dataset

# Extract column names of the samples
col_names <- colnames(samples)

# Split column names by "."
split_col_names <- strsplit(col_names, "\\.")

# Select relevant elements from split names (last seven elements, then remove the last three)
split_col_names_1 <- lapply(split_col_names, function(x) x[7:length(x)])  # Adjust this index for the new dataset structure if necessary
split_col_names_2 <- lapply(split_col_names_1, function(x) head(x, -3))  # Remove last three elements

# Create new column names by joining remaining elements
new_col_names <- sapply(split_col_names_2, function(x) paste(x, collapse = "."))

# Assign new column names to the samples dataframe
colnames(samples) <- new_col_names

# Recombine the genotypic marker info (first 9 columns) with the newly renamed sample columns
geno_2 <- as.data.frame(cbind(geno[, 1:9], samples))

## Remove markers with unknown chromosome/position
# Filter out rows where 'Pos' (position) is labeled as unknown (e.g., "Unk")
# Ensure the "Pos" column exists in the new dataset and adjust accordingly
geno_3 <- geno_2[geno_2$Pos != "Unk", , drop = FALSE]

# Identify duplicate markers by checking for duplicates in the 'New_Marker' column or the first row
duplicates <- duplicated(geno_3["New_Marker", ]) | duplicated(geno_3[1, ], fromLast = TRUE)

# Print out the logical vector showing which rows are duplicates
print(duplicates)

# Export the cleaned dataset to a CSV file
write.csv(geno_3, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/Pop80_Genotypic Matrix.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Adjust path accordingly
# Remove all columns except for Marker, REF, ALT, and sample columns


## Preparing the wide format for snpReady

# Read in the matrix file (generated previously)
matrix <- read.csv("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/Pop80_Genotypic Matrix_done.csv", header = TRUE)

# Remove unnecessary columns (e.g., REF, ALT) from the matrix if they exist
# Adjust the column names if the new dataset has different or additional columns
matrix_1 <- matrix[, !(names(matrix) %in% c("REF", "ALT"))]

# Transpose the matrix for snpReady
matrix_transpose <- t(matrix_1)

# Save the transposed matrix as a text file for snpReady
write.table(matrix_transpose, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/Pop80_snpReady matrix.txt", sep = "\t") # delete first row of text file

```

```{r}

#########################################################################
## Use snpReady to apply filtering and extract population summary metrics
#########################################################################

# Import the wide-format genotypic data
wide <- read.table("C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/Pop80_snpReady matrix.txt", head=T)

# Set the first column as row names and remove it from the data frame
row.names(wide) <- wide[,1]
wide <- wide[, -1]

## Remove non-informative markers
# Filtering markers with minimum allele frequency (MAF) of 0.001
inform_markers <- raw.data(data = as.matrix(wide), frame = "wide", base = FALSE, call.rate = 0, maf = .001, imput = FALSE)

# Print the report showing how many markers were removed
inform_markers$report

# Extract the cleaned marker matrix
inform_markers_matrix <- inform_markers$M.clean

# Transpose and export matrix
Matrix_transposed <- t(inform_markers_matrix)

write.csv(Matrix_transposed, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/TdGD80 Informative markers.csv", row.names = TRUE)

## Filter by call rate = 0.9
# Apply a call rate filter of 0.9 and MAF of 0.001
Callrate_0.9 <- raw.data(data = as.matrix(wide), frame = "wide", base = FALSE, call.rate = 0.9, maf = 0.001, imput = FALSE) 

# Print the report showing markers removed by MAF and call rate
Callrate_0.9$report

# Extract the matrix with filtered markers
Matrix_CR_0.9 <- Callrate_0.9$M.clean
dim(Matrix_CR_0.9) # Check the dimensions of the filtered matrix


## Population genetics analysis 
# Perform population genetics analysis on the filtered matrix
pop.gen <- popgen(M = Matrix_CR_0.9)

# Extract key components from the population genetics results
Markers_CR_0.9 <- pop.gen$whole$Markers
Genotypes_CR_0.9 <- data.frame(pop.gen$whole$Genotypes)
Population_CR_0.9 <- data.frame(pop.gen$whole$Population)
Variability_CR_0.9 <- data.frame(pop.gen$whole$Variability)


## Heterozygosity filtering
# Genotype heterozygosity
# Filter out individuals with heterozygosity levels above 0.2
individuals_to_remove <- rownames(Genotypes_CR_0.9)[Genotypes_CR_0.9$Ho > 0.2]
individuals_to_remove

# Remove these individuals from the genotypic matrix
Het_filter <- Matrix_CR_0.9[!rownames(Matrix_CR_0.9) %in% individuals_to_remove, ]

# Marker heterozygosity
# Filter out markers with heterozygosity levels above 0.1
markers_to_remove <- rownames(Markers_CR_0.9)[Markers_CR_0.9$Ho > 0.1]
length(markers_to_remove)

# Remove these markers from the genotypic matrix
Het_filter <- Het_filter[, !colnames(Het_filter) %in% markers_to_remove]

# Transpose and export matrix
Filtered_matrix <- t(Het_filter)

write.csv(Filtered_matrix, "C:/Users/addyhope/OneDrive - Kansas State University/23 TdGD/Analysis/Genotyping/Other pops/Pop80/Pop80_Filtered matrix_CR_Het.csv", row.names = TRUE)

```

```{r}

########################################################
## Plot histograms of MAF and heterozygosity
########################################################

# Define custom colors
col1 <- adjustcolor("#542788", alpha.f = 0.9)

# Minor allele frequency
hist(Markers_CR_0.9$MAF, breaks=seq(0,0.6, by=.025), main = "Pop 80 - Minor Allele Frequency", xlab = "Minor Allele Frequency (MAF)", ylab = "Number of markers", col = col1, border = "black")

# Marker heterozygosity
h_Ho <- hist(Markers_CR_0.9$Ho,
             breaks = seq(0, 0.8, by = 0.025),
             plot = FALSE)

hist(Markers_CR_0.9$Ho,
     breaks = seq(0, 0.8, by = 0.025),
     main = "Pop 80 - Marker Heterozygosity",
     xlab = "Marker Heterozygosity",
     ylab = "Number of markers",
     col = col1,
     border = "black",
     xlim = c(0, 0.6),
     ylim = c(0, max(h_Ho$counts) * 1.1))
# Optional threshold line
abline(v = 0.1, lty = 2)

## Individual Distribution
# Heterozygosity
hist(Genotypes_CR_0.9$Ho, 
     breaks = seq(0, 0.6, by = 0.025), 
     main = "Pop 80 - Individual Heterozygosity", 
     xlab = "Individual Heterozygosity", 
     ylab = "Number of WILs",
     col = col1, 
     border = "black")
# Add dashed vertical line at 0.2
abline(v = 0.2, lty = 2)

```
